frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment:
Meena: Aurat ki asli izzat tab aur badh jaati hai jab woh kisi ghar ki bahu aur bachchon ki maa banti hai. Aajkal ladkiyan bas apni pehchaan ki baat karti hain, par sach to yahi hai ki rishton se hi aurat ka maan banta hai.
Husband: Haan, baat to sahi hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman's worth and respect as primarily deriving from being a wife and mother rather than as an individual. It encodes relational framing and conditional respect tied to traditional family roles.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
